mainEntity.name: How much money will I get if I can only work part-time now?

acceptedAnswer.text: You should receive Temporary Partial Disability (TPD) benefits, which typically cover two-thirds of the difference between your old full-time pay and your new part-time earnings, helping to stabilize your income.
acceptedAnswer.description:
If you can only return to work part-time or in a lower-paying role due to your injury, you are eligible for Temporary Partial Disability (TPD) benefits. TPD is designed to 'bridge the gap.' In most cases, it pays two-thirds (66.6%) of the difference between your pre-injury weekly wage and your new, lower part-time earnings.

This ensures that you don't suffer a massive financial loss while attempting to return to the workforce.

- Calculation: (Pre-injury wage - Current part-time wage) x 0.66.
- Paid weekly or bi-weekly depending on your pay cycle.
- Requires submission of new paystubs to the insurance carrier.

Be sure to keep every paystub from your part-time work and send them immediately to the adjuster. If the insurance company stops paying the 'gap' money, you must file a motion for payment immediately to avoid falling behind on bills.
